prompt:
Design a **Training Assessment & Content Governance Backend** to run an offline-first fitness and skills evaluation program where participants complete structured plans, submit activity logs, earn rankings/certificates, and interact with moderated content. The backend, built with Koa for API delivery, Knex for data access, and PostgreSQL for persistence, exposes resource-grouped interfaces for authentication, user profiles, plans/tasks/logs, assessments, rankings, content items, moderation, campaigns, messaging, and data interchange. Assessment interfaces must support configurable scoring items (time in seconds, rep count, or combined completion) with per-item weights summing to 1.00; evaluation computation normalizes raw inputs to a 0–100 score using min/max bounds defined per item, applies weighted aggregation, and enforces outlier rules where any single log beyond 3 standard deviations from a user’s trailing 30 submissions is flagged and excluded unless manually approved. Attribution analysis must return dimensional breakdowns (e.g., strength/endurance/consistency), peer comparison percentiles by cohort, and full score-source traceability down to the originating log IDs and rule versions. Ranking supports level progression with clear thresholds (e.g., Bronze ≥60, Silver ≥75, Gold ≥90 over a rolling 14-day window) and certificate generation; each certificate includes a tamper-evident verification code computed from certificate ID, user ID, issue timestamp, and a locally stored secret, and verification interfaces must validate code integrity entirely offline. Operations & growth tools provide configuration of topics, rankings, and campaign pages with placement scheduling and phased rollout (start/end timestamps, 5%→25%→50%→100% ramps), coupon rules (fixed $5–$50 or 5%–30% off) and spend-threshold discounts (e.g., $100.00 unlocks 15% off), A/B test assignment using deterministic hashing on user ID, and event/conversion-funnel analytics captured via server-side event ingestion with idempotency keys.

Data models must include users, roles, permissions, resources, ACL entries, plans, tasks, activity_logs, assessment_rules (versioned), computed_scores, rankings, certificates, content_items, moderation_cases, appeals, campaigns, placements, coupons, messages, subscriptions, and import_jobs, with indexes on user_id, created_at, and composite uniqueness where required (e.g., username unique; certificate verification code unique; one active rule version per assessment type). Roles include Administrator, Operations Manager, Reviewer, Coach, and Participant. Access control combines RBAC with resource-level ACL for document read/download/edit/delete/share/submit/approve, supporting inheritance from folder/topic ownership and explicit deny overrides; every privileged action must write an immutable audit record (actor, action, resource, before/after hash, timestamp). The messaging center supports in-app messages only in offline mode (email/SMS channels must be disabled), templated notices for enrollment outcomes, waitlist promotions, schedule changes, and score releases, with read/unread tracking and subscription preferences; templates are versioned and rendered server-side with strict placeholder validation. Content moderation supports violation category configuration, automated pre-screening via deterministic local rules (keyword lists, file type allowlists, size limits, SHA-256 fingerprint checks), queue-based review with reviewer comments, and an appeal/re-review workflow with time limits (appeal allowed within 14 days of decision). Data import/export must support JSON/CSV with schema validation, backup/restore, consistency checks (foreign-key completeness, orphan detection), and conflict resolution (last-write-wins by updated_at with optional merge for append-only logs). Non-functional requirements: single-node Docker deployment, p95 API latency under 300 ms for common queries at 200 concurrent users, AES-256 encryption at rest for locally stored secrets and sensitive fields, password hashing with Argon2id (min 12 characters, lockout after 10 failed attempts for 15 minutes), structured logs and metrics stored locally, and full operation without any external network dependencies, third-party integrations, or external credentials.
